items.title: VI Live: gallery play op zorgeloze avond PSV, Reijnders slaat toe
items.summary: Op woensdagavond staan er opnieuw twee wedstrijden in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. PSV speelt in eigen huis tegen SC Heerenveen. NEC krijgt bezoek van FC Volendam. Volg de bekeravond in deze VI Live.

items.title: Manchester City v Newcastle United: Carabao Cup semi-final, second leg – live
items.summary: ⚽ Carabao Cup updates,, 8pm GMT kick-off (first leg: 2-0) ⚽ Live scoreboard | Follow us on Bluesky | And mail Scott 1 min: Trippier’s first act of the evening is a fine block of O’Reilly’s shot, the City man having drifted in from the left. Newcastle get the game started. They’re chasing down a two-goal deficit. Continue reading...

items.title: Schreuder koestert halve finale met NEC: 'Vooral niet gaan zweven'
items.summary: NEC is na woensdag halvefinalist in het KNVB Bekertoernooi en daar is ook alles mee gezegd. De Nijmegenaren versloegen FC Volendam met 1-0 door een doelpunt van Sami Ouaissa, waar aan Volendamse zijde nog wat over te doen was. NEC-trainer Dick Schreuder koestert de zege.

items.title: Sources: Unhappy Ronaldo to boycott 2nd game
items.summary: Al Nassr star Cristiano Ronaldo will maintain his stance and boycott a second-straight league game after not receiving a concrete guarantee that the Saudi Arabia PIF will make changes regarding the Saudi Pro League club's management, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Arsenal's Zubimendi rejects claim Madrid need him
items.summary: Arsenal midfielder Martín Zubimendi has denied speculation in Spain that he is the player that Real Madrid needs.

items.title: Newell's have dream plan to lure Messi in 2027
items.summary: Newell's Old Boys are working on a project to entice Lionel Messi to play for the club in the first half of 2027.

items.title: Real Madrid's big transfer questions: What to do about Vini Jr., Bellingham, Paz this summer?
items.summary: As usual, Real Madrid's January transfer window was quiet, but there are plenty of issues with the playing squad for the club to address in the summer.

items.title: Real Madrid's fitness guru loves 'pushing players to the limit.' Can he save their season?
items.summary: Madrid hope Antonio Pintus can put the players through a 'mini-preseason' that revives their campaign.

items.title: Volendammers bijten op tong over goal NEC
items.summary: FC Volendam strandde woensdag in het KNVB Bekertoernooi door met 1-0 te verliezen bij NEC. Om de enige goal van de wedstrijd was wat te doen. Bryan Linssen leek keeper Kayne van Oevelen te hinderen bij een hoge bal. De doelman liet de bal los en Sami Ouaissa tikte binnen. De Volendammers hielden zich na de wedstrijd in.

items.title: 'It doesn’t seem fair' - Rahm questions rules of LIV Golf's ranking boost
items.summary: Jon Rahm has welcomed LIV Golf League events finally being awarded world ranking points but believes "there's work to be done" to address a ruling that "doesn't seem fair".

items.title: 'Do I join Sky Sports?' Saints' Jordan discusses NFL future
items.summary: Cam Jordan is braced for uncharted territory as a free agent for the first time in his NFL career, but the New Orleans Saints stalwart insists he is not done sacking quarterbacks just yet.

items.title: Aderlating voor Scherpen: doelman geblesseerd naar de kant
items.summary: Kjell Scherpen is woensdagavond geblesseerd geraakt. De doelman van Union Sint-Gillis moest zich al na een kwart wedstrijd laten wisselen met een ogenschijnlijke bovenbeenblessure.

items.title: Droomseizoen NEC duurt voort: plek in halve finale beker na zege op FC Volendam
items.summary: NEC is halvefinalist in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. In eigen huis was een nipte zege op FC Volendam voldoende. De bezoekers deden zichzelf tekort na een uitstekende eerste helft, waardoor een curieuze goal van Sami Ouaissa de doorslag gaf.

items.title: Raducanu roars back from five games down to win in Transylvania
items.summary: Emma Raducanu overturned a five-game first-set deficit to book her place in the Transylvania Open quarter-finals with a straight‑sets victory over Kaja Juvan.

items.title: Rangers add a fourth vs Kilmarnock LIVE!
Littler targets even more Premier League records with nine-darters in his sights
items.summary: Luke Littler had a record-breaking campaign in the Premier League last year but he has his sights set on stepping things up even further in 2026.

items.title: Celtic’s match at Aberdeen postponed and Dundee v Motherwell also rained off
items.summary: Pitches at Pittodrie and Dens Park waterlogged Celtic lose chance to close gap after Hearts’ loss Celtic’s Scottish Premiership match at Aberdeen has been postponed, along with Dundee v Motherwell, after heavy rain led to the pitches becoming waterlogged. Aberdeen confirmed shortly before 4.30pm (GMT) that the Pittodrie pitch was not suitable for play. Celtic were hoping to close in on leaders Hearts and could have moved to within three points. Dundee have struggled with the Dens Park pitch over the past two years and it again fell victim to the heavy rain. This report will be updated … Continue reading...

items.title: Aberdeen v Celtic & Dundee v Motherwell games off
items.summary: Celtic's Scottish Premiership visit to Aberdeen is postponed, along with Dundee's match with Motherwell, after heavy rain in the north east.

items.title: Inside Man City's rebuild and plan for life without Guardiola
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at Manchester City's evolution and why the January window has offered renewed belief.

items.title: Football Daily | Arsenal’s fun boat sails on but quadruple attempt will surely hit the rocks
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Given their near misses in recent seasons, it is entirely understandable that most sentient Arsenal fans are not prepared to publicly entertain the notion that their team will almost certainly win the Premier League this season. While they’re all lying in bed at night secretly fantasising about Martin Ødegaard’s trophy lift, most remain too scarred by ridicule over perceived “bottle jobs” to confidently state that as far as the title is concerned, nothing can possibly go wrong. While they have gladly accepted Mikel Arteta’s invitation to jump on the fun boat, all are wearing life preservers. Quite what these same Arsenal fans make of various pundits blithely weighing up their chances of winning an unprecedented quadruple is anyone’s guess, but since they booked their place in the Fizzy Cup final, the external chatter has begun. Having won the square root of eff all in over five years, assorted experts are seriously suggesting Arsenal – Arsenal! – could win four shiny pots in the next four months. I have to feel sorry for the Ipswich fans who made plans to visit Fratton Park last night with the game being called off for the second time (frozen pitch previously, now waterlogged). Not sure how we are going to stop the next rearrangement if it doesn’t suit our knack-list but I hear floodlights do lose power sometimes” – Ben North (and no other devious Pompey fans). No disrespect to anyone – least of all Matt Atkinson – but comparing Timo Werner to the Yorkshire Stakhanovite that is James Milner (Monday’s Football Daily letters) is like comparing a pony to a thoroughbred” – Kev McCready. This is an extract from our daily football email … Football Daily. To get the full version, just visit this page and follow the instructions. Continue reading...
